role: A compact toggle control that displays the active projection lens and lets the user switch between projection bindings from a dropdown menu.
capabilities: Show active projection binding as a short label
Show live map-thing count with accessible title/aria-label
Toggle a dropdown menu of all projection bindings
Highlight the currently selected binding in the menu
Select a binding and notify parent via onPick
Close menu via scrim click or selection
Attach telemetry stamps to chip and each menu item
substance_why: It renders the full chip surface, menu, selection handling, animations, and accessibility annotations end-to-end, with only positioning logic abstracted to useChipMenu.
interactions_present: Click chip button toggles menu open/closed
Click menu item selects binding and closes menu
interactions_missing: No Escape key handler to close menu
No arrow-key navigation or focus trap inside menu
No handling for empty bindings list
No loading or error state for projection/count
No visible unit suffix decision for count (commented DNA aesthetic flag)
No explicit focus return to chip after close
performance_risks: AnimatePresence/motion re-renders and compositor work on every open/close
Menu re-renders and re-stamps all binding buttons on every parent render
No virtualization for large binding lists
encodeURIComponent + stamp string interpolation per binding per render
reuse_why: The lens-switching concept, short-label/count display, and binding selection logic are domain-correct, but the animation library, styling hooks, and accessibility need rebuilding.
decomposition_hints: Split the chip face (label + count) from the menu overlay (scrim + animated list)
Extract shortLabel into a shared projection-formatting utility
Wrap the motion menu into a reusable dropdown primitive
Move accessibility/keyboard behavior into a headless menu hook or component
Separate telemetry stamping from markup to avoid per-item render cost
